## Conversation History

User: I want to test your ability to follow instructions.
Assistant: Sure! Give me a specific format or constraint and I'll do my best.
User: Great, here comes the test.

## Tool Outputs

{"tool": "instruction_validator", "status": "ready", "message": "Will validate response against format constraints."}

## Question

Explain gravity to a child.

response_text:
Gravity is a gentle, invisible pull that all things have. Big things pull harder. Earth is very big, so it pulls you, your toys, and everything else toward it.

- That’s why things fall down when you drop them.
- It’s why you feel your weight and why your feet stay on the ground when you jump.
- Gravity keeps the air and oceans from floating away.
- It also keeps the Moon going around Earth and Earth going around the Sun.

Try a simple experiment: hold two different small toys at the same height and let them go at the same time (over something soft). You’ll see they hit the ground together. That’s gravity pulling on both of them in the same way.
